summary: AstraVault – a military-inspired secure personal command center dashboard. Full-stack app with React+Vite+TypeScript frontend (TailwindCSS, ShadCN/Radix UI) and Node.js+Express+MongoDB backend. Features JWT auth with HTTP-only cookies, email verification, 2FA OTP, account lockout, AES-256 encrypted private vault, custom link management, categorized Indian financial/government service links in sidebar, and a settings page. Matte black & military green UI with gold accents.
architectural_notes: Backend: Node.js + Express + MongoDB/Mongoose with Helmet, express-rate-limit, mongo-sanitize, xss-clean
Auth: JWT access token (15min) + refresh token (7 days) stored in HTTP-only secure cookies; token rotation on refresh
Vault encryption: AES-256-GCM with scrypt-derived key from vault password + userId salt
Frontend: React + Vite + TypeScript + TailwindCSS + Radix UI; Axios with auto refresh-token interceptor
Security: bcrypt salt 12, CORS restricted to frontend URL, rate limiting on login/register/vault routes
known_issues: Google Calendar widget is missing from the dashboard
Profile loading spinner never stops — infinite spinner reported after authentication, likely a race condition where isActorReady never becomes true or the profile query never resolves/errors
Login page and overall app structure were inadvertently removed/broken in Version 9; user requests restore to Version 4 baseline
App shows 'securing connection' screen — likely Internet Identity initialization hanging or HTTPS/network config issue blocking the II iframe from loading

existing_features.title: Implement Internet Identity-based authentication as the sole authentication mechanism. On first login, create a user profile in the backend with fields: name, username, email (optional), vaultPasswordHash, customLinks, createdAt. Store session state client-side via React Context. Protect all dashboard routes — redirect unauthenticated users to the login/landing page.

Build a vertical sidebar layout with the following categorized links. Each category and link must be rendered with an appropriate icon. Categories and links: BANK (State Bank of India – sbi.co.in, Punjab National Bank – pnbindia.in, India Post Payments Bank – ippbonline.com), EMAIL (Gmail – mail.google.com), INVESTMENT (CoinDCX – coindcx.com, ET Money – etmoney.com, Groww – groww.in), SERVICES (IRCTC – irctc.co.in, mAadhaar – maadhaar.uidai.gov.in, DigiLocker – digilocker.gov.in, LIC Digital – licindia.in, V380 Pro – v380.cn, Xiaomi Home – home.mi.com), PAYMENTS (Google Pay – pay.google.com, PhonePe – phonepe.com). Clicking a link opens the URL in a new tab.

Implement a custom link manager accessible from the sidebar under '+ ADD LINK'. Users can add a custom link with: name, URL, category (selectable from existing categories or a new one), and an icon (selectable from a predefined icon set). Custom links are stored per-user in the backend. Users can also edit and delete their custom links. Custom links appear in the sidebar under their chosen category.

Implement a Private Vault as a separate protected route (/vault). Access requires the user to enter a secondary vault password. The vault password is hashed (stored in the backend user record). Inside the vault, users can add, edit, delete, and search encrypted notes. Note content must be AES-256 encrypted before storage in the backend. Notes are decrypted client-side only after the correct vault password is entered. Provide a vault password reset option.

Build a Settings page (/settings) with the following sections: (1) Change login password (re-authenticate, then set new password with the same strength rules), (2) Change vault password (enter current vault password, then new vault password), (3) Enable/disable Email OTP 2FA (toggle with confirmation), (4) View active sessions list (principal ID + timestamp), (5) Logout all sessions button, (6) Delete account with confirmation dialog.

Add a profile dropdown in the top-right corner of the dashboard header. It must display the user's name/username and avatar initial. Dropdown options: Profile, Settings, Logout. Logout must show a confirmation modal before clearing the session.

Display a footer on the dashboard with the text 'Developed by Shubham Rathore' on the first line and 'Rajput – Creating History' on the second line. Style it with a subtle gold accent color consistent with the overall theme.

Design and enforce a cohesive military-inspired visual theme throughout the entire application: matte black (#0a0a0a / #111) background, deep military green (#2d4a1e / #3a5c28) as the primary accent color, gold (#c9a84c / #d4af37) as the highlight accent for active states, borders, and footer text. Use a clean sans-serif typeface. Apply shield and command-style iconography in the sidebar and dashboard header. Ensure the UI feels premium, minimal, and consistent across all pages.

Add a Google Calendar quick-access link entry in the EMAIL or SERVICES category of the default sidebar links in `frontend/src/data/defaultLinks.ts`. The link should be named 'Google Calendar', point to 'https://calendar.google.com', use an appropriate calendar icon from the available icon set, and open in a new tab — consistent with how all other default links are rendered in the sidebar.

Investigate and fix the profile loading issue in ProtectedRoute and useQueries hooks. Ensure that after Internet Identity authentication, the user profile is fetched reliably from the backend. Handle edge cases: (1) actor not yet initialized when profile query fires, (2) query firing before the identity is fully resolved, (3) missing or null profile response not correctly distinguished from a loading state. Add appropriate loading skeleton/spinner while the profile is being fetched and display a meaningful error state if the fetch fails, allowing the user to retry.
In `frontend/src/data/defaultLinks.ts`, update the URL of the 'State Bank of India' entry in the BANK category from its current value to 'https://sbi.bank.in/'.

In `frontend/src/data/defaultLinks.ts`, update the URL of the 'mAadhaar' entry in the SERVICES category to 'https://uidai.gov.in/en/' and update the URL of the 'Xiaomi Home' entry in the SERVICES category to 'http://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.xiaomi.smarthome&hl=en_IN'. Both links must continue to open in a new tab.

Diagnose and fix the profile loading failure in ProtectedRoute and useQueries hooks. Ensure the actor is fully initialized and the Internet Identity is resolved before the getProfile query fires. Distinguish between 'profile not found' (new user → show ProfileSetupModal) and a genuine fetch error (show error state with retry). Add a loading skeleton/spinner while the profile is in flight. Ensure the React Query cache key for the profile query is invalidated and re-fetched whenever the authenticated principal changes.

Rewrite the `useGetProfile` query in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` so it: (1) is only enabled when `isActorReady` is `true`, (2) uses the authenticated principal as part of the React Query cache key so the query is automatically invalidated and re-fetched when the principal changes, (3) correctly distinguishes a `null` / empty optional response (profile not found → new user) from a thrown error (genuine fetch failure).

Rewrite `frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx` to use the fixed `useGetProfile` query. Show a full-page loading skeleton while `isActorReady` is `false` or the profile query is in flight. Show a meaningful error state with a 'Retry' button if the profile fetch fails. Redirect to the login page only when the user is definitively unauthenticated (not during loading).

Audit and rewrite `frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts` (without modifying the immutable `frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts` path — instead patch the internal logic exposed to consumers) so that `isActorReady` is only set to `true` after both (a) the Internet Identity principal is fully resolved and non-anonymous, and (b) the actor instance is confirmed non-null. Expose `isActorReady` as a stable boolean via the hook's return value. If the file is immutable, create a thin `useActorReady.ts` wrapper hook in `frontend/src/hooks/` that derives and exports `isActorReady` from the existing `useActor` hook.

Rewrite `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` — specifically the `useGetProfile` query — so that: (1) the query's `enabled` flag is strictly gated on `isActorReady === true`; (2) the React Query cache key includes the authenticated principal string (e.g. `['profile', principalText]`) so it auto-invalidates when the principal changes; (3) a `null` / empty optional returned by the backend is treated as 'profile not found' (new user flow) and not as an error; (4) only a thrown exception is treated as a genuine fetch failure. All other query hooks in the file must also gate their `enabled` flag on `isActorReady`.

Rewrite `frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx` to implement the following strict state machine: (1) While `isActorReady` is `false` OR the profile query `isLoading` is `true` → render a full-page military-themed loading skeleton/spinner (no redirect, no children). (2) If the profile query `isError` is `true` → render a full-page error state with a gold-accented 'Retry' button that calls `refetch()`. (3) If the user is definitively unauthenticated (identity hook confirms not logged in AND actor initialization is complete) → redirect to `/login`. (4) If `data` is `null` (profile not found, new user) → render `<ProfileSetupModal>` without redirecting. (5) If profile data is present → render `<Outlet />` / children. Eliminate any race condition where the component redirects to login during the loading phase.

Audit `frontend/src/App.tsx` — specifically the `AuthGuard` component — to ensure it does not redirect unauthenticated or 'still-initializing' users to `/login` before the Internet Identity `isInitializing` / `isLoading` flag has settled to `false`. The guard must wait for initialization to complete before making any routing decision. If `isInitializing` is `true`, render the same full-page loading skeleton used in `ProtectedRoute`.

Audit `frontend/src/hooks/useActorReady.ts` to confirm it correctly derives `isActorReady` as `true` only when: (a) the Internet Identity `isInitializing` flag is `false`, (b) the principal is non-anonymous, and (c) the actor instance returned by `useActor` is non-null and not in a fetching state. If any of these conditions are incorrectly evaluated (e.g., `isInitializing` is never read, anonymous principal is not filtered out, or actor fetch status is ignored), rewrite the hook so all three conditions are strictly enforced before `isActorReady` flips to `true`.

Audit `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` — specifically the `useGetProfile` query — and verify the `enabled` flag is strictly `isActorReady === true`. If the query is currently firing before `isActorReady` is `true` (causing it to hang or never resolve), fix the `enabled` condition. Also ensure that a `null` / empty optional returned by the backend `getProfile` is treated as 'profile not found' (new user flow) and NOT as an error or infinite loading state. A thrown exception must be the only condition treated as `isError`. All other queries in `useQueries.ts` that call backend functions must also gate their `enabled` flag on `isActorReady`.

Rewrite `frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx` to implement a strict state machine that eliminates the infinite spinner: (1) While `isActorReady` is `false` OR the profile query `isLoading` is `true` → render a full-page military-themed loading spinner (no redirect, no children). (2) If `isError` is `true` → render a full-page error state with a gold-accented 'Retry' button that calls `refetch()`. (3) If the user is definitively unauthenticated (not logged in AND actor initialization is complete) → redirect to `/login`. (4) If `data` is `null` (profile not found, new user) → render `<ProfileSetupModal>` without redirecting. (5) If profile data is present → render `<Outlet />` / children. The component must never redirect to `/login` during the loading phase.

Restore `frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx` to a fully functional login page matching the Version 4 design: AstraVault branding, military-themed matte black background with gold and military-green accents, and a single 'Login with Internet Identity' button wired to the `useInternetIdentity` hook. The page must be accessible at the `/login` route for unauthenticated users.

Restore `frontend/src/App.tsx` to the Version 4 routing structure with the following routes: `/login` (public, LoginPage), `/` (protected, DashboardPage), `/vault` (protected, VaultPage), `/settings` (protected, SettingsPage). The `AuthGuard` must wait for Internet Identity `isInitializing` to settle to `false` before making any routing decision — rendering the full-page military-themed loading spinner in the interim. Unauthenticated users must be redirected to `/login` only after initialization is confirmed complete.

Restore `frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx` to the Version 4 strict state machine: (1) While `isActorReady` is `false` OR the profile query `isLoading` is `true` → render a full-page military-themed loading spinner (no redirect, no children). (2) If `isError` is `true` → render a full-page error state with a gold-accented 'Retry' button that calls `refetch()`. (3) If the user is definitively unauthenticated (not logged in AND actor initialization is complete) → redirect to `/login`. (4) If profile data is `null` (new user) → render `<ProfileSetupModal>` without redirecting. (5) If profile data is present → render `<Outlet />` / children. The component must never redirect to `/login` during the loading phase.

Restore `frontend/src/hooks/useActorReady.ts` so that `isActorReady` is `true` only when: (a) the Internet Identity `isInitializing` flag is `false`, (b) the principal is non-anonymous, and (c) the actor instance returned by `useActor` is non-null. All three conditions must be strictly enforced.

Restore `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` so the `useGetProfile` query: (1) has its `enabled` flag strictly gated on `isActorReady === true`; (2) includes the authenticated principal string in the React Query cache key (e.g. `['profile', principalText]`); (3) treats a `null` / empty optional returned by the backend as 'profile not found' (new user flow), not as an error; (4) treats only a thrown exception as a genuine fetch failure (`isError`). All other query hooks in the file must also gate their `enabled` flag on `isActorReady`.

Restore the full dashboard layout matching Version 4: `DashboardLayout.tsx` composing `DashboardHeader.tsx`, `AppSidebar.tsx`, the main content area, and `DashboardFooter.tsx`. The header must include the logo, sidebar toggle, and profile dropdown (name/username/avatar initial, with Profile / Settings / Logout options and a logout confirmation modal). The footer must display 'Developed by Shubham Rathore' on the first line and 'Rajput – Creating History' on the second line in gold accent. All pages (Dashboard, Vault, Settings) must render inside this layout.

Restore `frontend/src/components/AppSidebar.tsx` to the Version 4 design: vertical collapsible sidebar with categorized default links and user custom links grouped by category, each with appropriate Lucide icons. The sidebar must include a '+ ADD LINK' entry that opens the `CustomLinkManager` modal. Active link state must be highlighted with the gold accent color. The sidebar must use the military theme (dark background, military-green category headers, gold active/hover states).

Ensure `frontend/src/data/defaultLinks.ts` contains all default sidebar links with the following corrected URLs (preserving all other Version 4 entries and their icons): State Bank of India → `https://sbi.bank.in/`, mAadhaar → `https://uidai.gov.in/en/`, Xiaomi Home → `http://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.xiaomi.smarthome&hl=en_IN`, Google Calendar (EMAIL or SERVICES category) → `https://calendar.google.com`. All other default links (Punjab National Bank, India Post Payments Bank, Gmail, CoinDCX, ET Money, Groww, IRCTC, DigiLocker, LIC Digital, V380 Pro, Google Pay, PhonePe) must remain unchanged. All links must open in a new tab.

Restore the military-inspired visual theme across all pages and components to match Version 4 exactly: matte black (#0a0a0a / #111) background, deep military green (#2d4a1e / #3a5c28) as primary accent, gold (#c9a84c / #d4af37) for active states, borders, and footer text. Fonts: Rajdhani / Inter. Ensure `frontend/src/index.css` and `frontend/tailwind.config.js` define these tokens and that all pages (Login, Dashboard, Vault, Settings) consistently apply the theme.

In `frontend/src/App.tsx`, ensure the `AuthGuard` component does not attempt to render or redirect while the Internet Identity `isInitializing` flag is `true`. While `isInitializing` is `true`, render only the full-page military-themed loading spinner. No routing decision (redirect to `/login` or render protected content) must be made until `isInitializing` has definitively settled to `false`. This prevents the UI from appearing stuck at the 'Securing Connection' screen.

Audit `frontend/src/hooks/useActorReady.ts` to ensure `isActorReady` remains `false` while Internet Identity is still initializing (`isInitializing === true`). The hook must not flip `isActorReady` to `true` prematurely before the II client has finished resolving the identity. All three conditions must be enforced in order: (a) `isInitializing` is `false`, (b) the principal is non-anonymous, (c) the actor instance is non-null.

Audit `frontend/src/App.tsx` — specifically the `AuthGuard` component — and ensure it does not make any routing decision (redirect to `/login` or render protected content) while `isInitializing` is `true`. While `isInitializing` is `true`, render only the full-page military-themed loading spinner. Only after `isInitializing` has definitively settled to `false` may the guard redirect unauthenticated users to `/login` or render protected routes.

Audit `frontend/src/hooks/useActorReady.ts` and enforce that `isActorReady` is `true` only when all three conditions are met in order: (a) `isInitializing` from `useInternetIdentity` is `false`, (b) the principal is non-anonymous (not equal to `Principal.anonymous()`), and (c) the actor instance returned by `useActor` is non-null. If the hook currently sets `isActorReady` to `true` before `isInitializing` has settled, or before the principal is confirmed non-anonymous, or before the actor is non-null, rewrite it to enforce all three conditions strictly.

Restore the Version 4 color palette in `frontend/src/index.css` and `frontend/tailwind.config.js`: matte black (#0a0a0a / #111) for all page backgrounds, deep military green (#2d4a1e / #3a5c28) as the primary accent color, and gold (#c9a84c / #d4af37) for active states, borders, hover highlights, and footer text. Ensure all CSS custom property tokens and Tailwind theme extensions exactly match the Version 4 values. Do not change any layout, functionality, routing, or component logic.

Restore `frontend/src/App.tsx` to the Version 4 routing structure: `/login` (public, LoginPage), `/` (protected, DashboardPage), `/vault` (protected, VaultPage), `/settings` (protected, SettingsPage). The `AuthGuard` must render only the full-page military-themed loading spinner while `isInitializing` is `true` and must never redirect to `/login` or render protected content until `isInitializing` has definitively settled to `false`.

Restore `frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx` to the Version 4 design: AstraVault branding, military-themed matte black background with gold and military-green accents, and a single 'Login with Internet Identity' button wired to the `useInternetIdentity` hook. Already-authenticated users must be redirected to the dashboard.

Restore `frontend/src/hooks/useActorReady.ts` so `isActorReady` is `true` only when all three conditions are met in order: (a) `isInitializing` from `useInternetIdentity` is `false`, (b) the principal is non-anonymous (not equal to `Principal.anonymous()`), and (c) the actor instance returned by `useActor` is non-null. All three conditions must be strictly enforced.

Restore the full dashboard layout to Version 4: `DashboardLayout.tsx` composing `DashboardHeader.tsx`, `AppSidebar.tsx`, main content area, and `DashboardFooter.tsx`. The header must include the AstraVault logo, sidebar toggle, and profile dropdown (name/username/avatar initial) with Profile, Settings, and Logout options — Logout shows a confirmation modal before clearing session. The footer must display 'Developed by Shubham Rathore' on the first line and 'Rajput – Creating History' on the second line in gold accent. All pages (Dashboard, Vault, Settings) must render inside this layout.

Restore `frontend/src/data/defaultLinks.ts` with all Version 4 default sidebar links and the following corrected URLs: State Bank of India → `https://sbi.bank.in/`, mAadhaar → `https://uidai.gov.in/en/`, Xiaomi Home → `http://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.xiaomi.smarthome&hl=en_IN`, Google Calendar (in EMAIL or SERVICES category) → `https://calendar.google.com`. All other links (Punjab National Bank – `https://pnbindia.in`, India Post Payments Bank – `https://ippbonline.com`, Gmail – `https://mail.google.com`, CoinDCX – `https://coindcx.com`, ET Money – `https://etmoney.com`, Groww – `https://groww.in`, IRCTC – `https://irctc.co.in`, DigiLocker – `https://digilocker.gov.in`, LIC Digital – `https://licindia.in`, V380 Pro – `https://v380.cn`, Google Pay – `https://pay.google.com`, PhonePe – `https://phonepe.com`) must remain unchanged. All links open in a new tab.

Restore the Version 4 military-inspired visual theme across all pages and components: matte black (#0a0a0a / #111) for all page backgrounds, deep military green (#2d4a1e / #3a5c28) as the primary accent color, gold (#c9a84c / #d4af37) for active states, borders, hover highlights, and footer text. Fonts: Rajdhani and Inter. Ensure `frontend/src/index.css` and `frontend/tailwind.config.js` define these exact CSS custom property tokens and Tailwind theme extensions. All pages (Login, Dashboard, Vault, Settings) must consistently apply the theme.

Restore `frontend/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx` to the Version 4 design: welcome banner, stat cards (default links count, custom links count, vault notes count), and quick-access grid for navigating to app sections, all styled with the military theme.

Restore `frontend/src/pages/VaultPage.tsx` to the Version 4 design: vault unlock screen requiring the secondary vault password, and once unlocked, a full CRUD + search interface for AES-256-GCM encrypted notes. Notes are encrypted client-side before storage and decrypted client-side after unlock. Include a vault password reset option. All styled with the military theme.

Restore `frontend/src/pages/SettingsPage.tsx` to the Version 4 design with these sections: (1) Update profile (name, username, email), (2) Change vault password (enter current vault password, then new vault password), (3) Enable/disable Email OTP 2FA toggle, (4) View active sessions list (principal ID + timestamp), (5) Logout all sessions button, (6) Delete account with confirmation dialog. All styled with the military theme.

Restore `frontend/src/components/ProfileSetupModal.tsx` to the Version 4 design: full-screen modal for new users to register a profile with name, username, optional email, and vault password (SHA-256 hashed before submission). On success, invalidate the profile query cache so ProtectedRoute can proceed to render the dashboard.

Restore `frontend/src/components/CustomLinkManager.tsx` to the Version 4 design: dialog-based UI for adding, editing, and deleting custom links. Fields: name, URL, category (selectable from existing categories or a new custom one), icon (selectable from a predefined icon set). Changes are persisted to the backend and reflected immediately in the sidebar. All styled with the military theme.

Restore `frontend/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx` to a fully functional, visually complete login page with AstraVault branding: matte black (#0a0a0a) background, gold (#c9a84c / #d4af37) and military-green (#2d4a1e / #3a5c28) accents, shield/command iconography, Rajdhani/Inter fonts, and a single 'Login with Internet Identity' button wired to the `useInternetIdentity` hook's `login` function. Already-authenticated users (identity resolved and non-anonymous) must be automatically redirected to `/`. The page must be accessible at the `/login` route for all unauthenticated users.

Restore `frontend/src/hooks/useActorReady.ts` so that `isActorReady` is `true` only when all three conditions are strictly met in order: (a) `isInitializing` from `useInternetIdentity` is `false`, (b) the principal is non-anonymous (not equal to `Principal.anonymous()`), and (c) the actor instance returned by `useActor` is non-null. The hook must never flip `isActorReady` to `true` prematurely. Export `isActorReady` as a stable boolean.

Restore `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` so the `useGetProfile` query: (1) has its `enabled` flag strictly gated on `isActorReady === true`; (2) includes the authenticated principal string in the React Query cache key (e.g. `['profile', principalText]`) so it auto-invalidates when the principal changes; (3) treats a `null` / empty optional returned by the backend as 'profile not found' (new user flow), not as an error; (4) treats only a thrown exception as a genuine fetch failure (`isError`). All other query hooks in the file that call backend functions must also gate their `enabled` flag on `isActorReady`.

Restore `frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx` to the strict state machine: (1) While `isActorReady` is `false` OR the profile query `isLoading` is `true` → render a full-page military-themed loading spinner (no redirect, no children rendered). (2) If `isError` is `true` → render a full-page error state with a gold-accented 'Retry' button that calls `refetch()`. (3) If the user is definitively unauthenticated (not logged in AND actor initialization is complete) → redirect to `/login`. (4) If `data` is `null` (profile not found, new user) → render `<ProfileSetupModal>` without redirecting. (5) If profile data is present → render `<Outlet />` / children. The component must never redirect to `/login` during the loading phase.

Restore `frontend/src/App.tsx` to the Version 4 routing structure with routes: `/login` (public, LoginPage), `/` (protected, DashboardPage), `/vault` (protected, VaultPage), `/settings` (protected, SettingsPage). The `AuthGuard` component must render only the full-page military-themed loading spinner while `isInitializing` is `true` and must never redirect to `/login` or render protected content until `isInitializing` has definitively settled to `false`.

Restore `frontend/src/components/ProfileSetupModal.tsx` to the Version 4 design: a full-screen modal for new users to register a profile with fields for name, username, optional email, and vault password (SHA-256 hashed client-side before submission). On successful registration, invalidate the `['profile', principalText]` React Query cache key so `ProtectedRoute` immediately transitions from the modal to the dashboard. Style the modal with the military theme (matte black background, gold accents, military-green buttons).

new_feature_requests.summary: JWT authentication with access/refresh tokens in HTTP-only cookies, email verification (15min expiry), account lock after 5 failed attempts
Email 2FA OTP support during login flow
AES-256 encrypted private vault for notes with secondary vault password, CRUD + search
Sidebar with categorized Indian financial/government links (SBI, PNB, IRCTC, DigiLocker, Groww, etc.)
Custom link management: add/edit/delete links with category and icon selection
Settings page: change login/vault password, enable 2FA, view/logout sessions, delete account
Google Calendar OAuth 2.0 integration with event fetch, add, and sync widget on dashboard
Dockerfile and .env.example for production deployment

Implement backend data models in Motoko for: User (principal, name, username, emailVerified, vaultPasswordHash, failedLoginAttempts, lockUntil, refreshTokens array, customLinks array, createdAt), CustomLink (id, name, url, category, iconName, userId), and VaultNote (id, encryptedContent, userId, createdAt). Expose CRUD endpoints for each model, protected by caller principal verification.

Audit the backend `getProfile` Motoko function to ensure it returns a well-formed optional profile response for the authenticated caller principal. Confirm it does not return an error or trap for a principal that has no profile, and that it correctly distinguishes between 'profile not found' and an actual error condition.
Update mAadhaar sidebar link URL to https://uidai.gov.in/en/ and Xiaomi Home link URL to http://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.xiaomi.smarthome&hl=en_IN

Audit and rewrite the `useActor` hook to guarantee the authenticated ICP actor is fully initialized before any query dependent on it can fire. The hook must expose a stable `isActorReady` boolean that is only `true` once the actor instance is non-null and the Internet Identity principal is resolved. All queries that call backend functions must have `enabled` gated on `isActorReady`.

Audit `backend/main.mo` — specifically the `getProfile` function — to ensure it returns a well-formed `?UserProfile` optional for the caller principal: returning `null` when no profile exists and only trapping or returning an error variant for genuine runtime failures. It must never trap on a missing profile.

Audit `backend/main.mo` — specifically the `getProfile` function — to confirm it returns `null` (empty optional `?UserProfile`) when no profile exists for the caller principal, and only traps or returns an error for genuine runtime failures. If it currently traps on a missing profile or returns an error variant for 'not found', fix it to return `null` instead. Do not change any other backend logic or data structures.

Audit `backend/main.mo` — specifically the `getProfile` function — to confirm it returns `null` (empty optional `?UserProfile`) when no profile exists for the caller principal, and only traps or returns an error for genuine runtime failures. If it currently traps on a missing profile, fix it to return `null` instead. Do not change any other backend logic or data structures.

Audit `frontend/src/App.tsx` — specifically the `AuthGuard` component — to confirm it does not redirect users to `/login` while `isInitializing` is `true`. If it currently makes a routing decision before initialization is complete, fix it to render the full-page military-themed loading skeleton (same as `ProtectedRoute`) until `isInitializing` settles to `false`.

Restore the app to version 4 baseline: login page, authentication flow, sidebar, dashboard, and military look must match version 4 exactly. Only apply the link updates (URLs) that were previously requested. No other changes.
